commit.message:
Remove JS text truncation in table formatters, let CSS line-clamp handle it

Removed the 60-char details truncation and 24-char secret truncation from
JS formatters — CSS 3-line clamp handles visual truncation, and the removed
JS truncation was preventing tooltip overflow detection from triggering.
Also improved tooltip handler with dual detection: JS truncation check
(tip length vs visible text) and CSS clamp check (unset clamp to measure
true scrollHeight).

then compares against clamped height to decide if tooltip is needed.

Fix graph ordering: WEB01 after DC01, access→host edges

Pivot edges now prefer host→host (not access→host) so BFS layer
assignment places pivot destinations after the source host. Added
unconditional access→host edges so the chain flows correctly:
cred → access → host → [pivot] → next host.

commit.message:
Add via_access_id provenance tracking to kill-chain graph

Schema v3→v4 migration adds via_access_id FK on credentials and vulns
tables, linking discoveries to the access session that found them.
State-server add_credential/add_vuln accept the new parameter.
State-viewer graph uses via_access_id for explicit edge inference
(access→vuln, access→cred) with heuristic fallback for NULL values.
Access nodes are pre-created before vuln/cred processing so references
resolve correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix provided-cred chain ordering and reduce edge crossings

Provided-cred chain detection was fragile — parsed node labels to
find hosts. Now tracks hostsViaProvidedCred directly during access
node creation when matching against a provided credential. This
ensures attacker->cred->access->host->downstream ordering instead
of cred appearing as a sibling of the host.

Also broadened cred->access matching to check access.method for
the credential username (catches cases where username formats
differ between cred and access records).

Added barycenter heuristic for node ordering within layers (3
passes). Nodes in each layer are sorted by the average Y position
of their parent nodes, which reduces edge crossings — e.g., the
DC->gMSA_ADCS edge no longer has to route through credential boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix kill-chain ordering: provided creds flow attacker->cred->access->host

When a credential is provided (source matches "pentest", "provided",
etc.), the chain now flows: Attacker -> cred -> access -> host ->
downstream findings. Previously the host got a direct edge from
Attacker, making the cred appear as a sibling instead of an ancestor.

Changes:
- Skip attacker->host edge for hosts reachable via provided-cred chain
- Add access->host bridge edges so downstream nodes (vulns, pivots,
  blocked) that connect from the host node stay in the chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Prune dead-end vulns from graph, link provided creds from Attacker

Graph now only shows vulns that chain into something downstream
(credential, access). Dead-end findings that didn't lead anywhere
are pruned from the graph but remain in the Vulns table.

Credentials whose source matches "provided", "scope", "pentest",
"given", "initial", or "pre-engagement" now link from the Attacker
node instead of from a host — reflecting that they were supplied
as part of scope, not discovered through exploitation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Render pivots as labeled edges instead of nodes

Pivots are relationships, not entities — they now render as styled
edges between source access/host and destination host. Edge label
shows method + status (e.g. "sshuttle", "chisel (identified)").
Exploited pivots are solid green, identified are dashed yellow,
blocked are dotted red. Removes the uninformative column of
identical "pivot" boxes.

Edge legend labels updated: Exploited/Identified/Blocked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve kill-chain graph: subtitles, legend, manual refresh

Graph nodes now show two lines — label + contextual subtitle:
- Hosts: IP + role/OS
- Vulns: title + severity (color-coded by severity level)
- Credentials: user + secret_type (cracked)
- Access: user@host + type|privilege
- Pivots: method + source->destination (instead of generic "pivot")
- Blocked: technique + truncated reason

Adds a legend bar below the graph showing node shapes/colors and
edge styles (confirmed/pending/blocked).

SSE auto-refresh now only updates cards + tables. Graph only redraws
on explicit Refresh button click to prevent disruptive reloads.

commit.message:
Add state-viewer web dashboard with kill-chain graph

Read-only browser dashboard for engagement/state.db served at localhost:8099.
Stdlib-only Python HTTP server with inline HTML/CSS/JS — no dependencies.

Features:
- Kill-chain SVG attack graph (hosts, vulns, creds, access, pivots, blocked)
- State tables with sorting, filtering, collapsible sections
- SSE live updates (events every 2s, full refresh every 10s)
- Token-based auth for remote access (generate-token.sh binds 0.0.0.0)
- HMAC-signed session cookies, Bearer header support for API clients
- Graceful degradation when DB doesn't exist yet

Closes #64

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dashboard reliability fixes, JSONL agent discovery, event watcher timeout

- Fix crash on embedded null characters in agent output
- Fix stale agents reappearing on dashboard restart (5-min mtime filter)

- Add credential-cracking-agent and pivoting-agent to SubagentStop hook

commit.message:
feat: intelligent clip selection engine — data-driven scoring

New utils/clip_scorer.py scores clips 0-100 across 5 dimensions:
- Topic velocity (0-25): matches against daily_signals.json topics
- Engagement potential (0-20): historical X performance by topic
- Novelty (0-20): channels not featured in recent episodes score higher
- Speaker authority (0-15): Tier 1 channels score 15, Tier 2 score 10
- Emotional impact (0-20): breaking/shocking/unprecedented keyword detection
- Live stream topics get 1.5x velocity boost per LIVE_INTELLIGENCE_LAWS

Wired into clip_selector.py: after Claude selects + filters candidates,
clip_scorer.rank_clips() re-ranks by data-driven score before final selection.

commit.message:
SLM-43: Camunda 8 migration, monorepo restructure, and onboarding presentation (#38)

* SLM-43: Camunda 8 migration with 39 JSON forms and monorepo restructuring

- Migrate onboarding BPMN from Camunda 7 to Camunda 8 (v6-c8)
  - camunda: namespace → zeebe: extension elements
  - candidateGroups → zeebe:assignmentDefinition
  - formKey → zeebe:formDefinition with formId
  - decisionRef → zeebe:calledDecision
  - camunda:properties → zeebe:properties
  - isExecutable=false → true
- Create 39 Camunda 8 JSON form files (.form, schema v16)
  - SP1: 5 forms (review, leverage, gather, submit, triage)
  - SP2: 2 forms (analysis, backlog)
  - SP3: 8 forms (tech arch, security, risk, financial, vendor landscape, DD, evaluate, AI governance)
  - SP4: 6 forms (refine, PoC, tech risk, negotiate, finalize, build reqs)
  - PDLC: 4 forms (arch review, development, testing, integration)
  - SP5: 4 forms (UAT, approval, onboard, close)
  - Vendor: 10 forms (intake through close)
- Add reusable migration script (scripts/migrate-c7-to-c8.py)
- Create intake form field-to-role mapping (111 fields, 13 roles, 4 forms)
- Restructure repo: framework/ (strategic IP) + customers/fs-onboarding/ (implementation)
- Update project config to reflect Camunda 8 as target engine

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* SLM-43: Rebuild presentation as onboarding-only and complete monorepo restructure

- Rebuild docs/presentations/index.html as 33-slide onboarding-only presentation
- Archive strategic 8-phase slides to framework/docs/presentations/strategic-esg-framework.html
- Add build script (scripts/build-onboarding-presentation.py) for deterministic HTML assembly
- Move strategic SVGs/screenshots to framework/docs/presentations/
- Move customer DMN tables to customers/fs-onboarding/processes/dmn/
- Move customer docs, scripts, and presentations to customers/fs-onboarding/
- Move old BPMN versions to customers/fs-onboarding/processes/archive/
- Move framework docs/requirements to framework/docs/requirements/
- Update CLAUDE.md, agents, rules, and memory bank for new paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* SLM-43: Address PR review findings

- Remove dead code in migration script (no-op transform_receive)
- Stage OB-DMN files under customers/fs-onboarding/processes/dmn/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* SLM-43: Add onboarding presentation auth worker

Cloudflare Worker for OTP-protected access to the vendor onboarding
presentation at onboarding.agentic-innovations.com. Uses Descope for
authentication with email allowlist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Avalon engine — hidden role social deduction for S3

6-player (4 good / 2 evil) implementation of The Resistance: Avalon.
5-phase state machine: discuss → nominate → vote → quest → assassinate.
Roles: Merlin, Percival, Loyal×2 / Assassin, Morgana (5-8p configs).
Scoring: good win +3, evil win +4, assassination bonus +2, Merlin survival +2.
61 tests passing. God-mode spectator on :8811.

Also fixes qwen3-8b thinking mode timeout issue by disabling CoT via
extra_body in the OpenRouter adapter call.

commit.message:
Engine coupling + expansion — CX-11, CX-12, CX-20, CX-23, CX-33 + registration of CX-32–35 (#90)

Phase 1: CX-32, CX-33, CX-34, CX-35 registered in TASK-ARCHITECTURE.md and whiteboard.md.
CX-11 and CX-12 confirmed as DONE (790-line and 502-line working drafts on disk from prior
unlogged runs). TASK-ARCHITECTURE.md updated to CX-00A → CX-35. docs/ README expanded.
docs/cx-dependency-graph.md updated with 4 new nodes and current status.

Phase 2 (CX-20): sql/008-room-schema-extension.sql — 4 tables (rooms, room_visits,
room_votes, bloom_history), RLS policies, indexes, 1,680-row population from zip_metadata.
CX-24 (Bloom State Engine) and CX-27 (Superscript/Subscript) newly unblocked.

Phase 3 (CX-23): scripts/build-navigation-graph.py + middle-math/navigation-graph.json.
1,680 nodes × 4 directional edges = 6,720 edges. All nodes pass Type Exclusion Rule
validation (hub Type not repeated in any neighbor, all 4 neighbor Types distinct).

Phase 4 (CX-33): scripts/build-dashboard-data.py + docs/dashboard/index.html +
docs/dashboard/data/progress.json. Dark-theme static HTML, no external dependencies.
42-cell deck grid, CX counter, 8-section Negotiosum summary. Reads repo state on each run.

Phase 5: NEXT-ROUND-HANDOFF.md replaced — CX-14 now critical path (all 5 blockers met).

Negotiosum validation: 5/5 checks passed.
No card content changed. No SCL rules modified.

commit.message:
fix: distribution decimal mapping bug for mixed-decimal pairs (BASE)

When user entered stablecoin as token0 (e.g. USDC/NOX), the invert_price
decimal swap assumed token0=volatile, token1=stable — causing 10^12x
inflated liquidity on mixed-decimal chains (6/18 dec). Now uses
token1_is_stable flag to determine correct mapping.

fix(runner): prevent hash collisions from primitive deduplication in createRef (#2995)

globalThis.commontools for interactive debugging of piece data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

console commands reference, including agent-browser usage examples.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(runner): prevent primitive deduplication in createRef hash

The `seen` Set in createRef's traverse function was intended for cycle
detection on object references, but it also added primitives (strings,
numbers, booleans). Since JavaScript's Set uses value equality for
primitives, repeated strings like "primary" and "secondary" were
treated as "already seen" and replaced with null.

This caused patterns that differed only in which position held a
repeated value (e.g. which button has variant="primary") to hash
identically, making setupInternal skip re-instantiation and producing
writes=0 — the DOM never updated.

The fix restricts the seen Set to objects, arrays, and functions, which
are the only types that can form reference cycles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

from `readArgumentCell` (it delegates to readCell without awaiting).

commit.message:
feat: use iOS Vision framework for precise card rectangle detection

- Add native Swift MethodChannel (CardDetectorChannel) using
  VNDetectRectanglesRequest to detect card rectangles in captured images
- Add Flutter CardDetectorService to call native Vision detection
- Crop now uses Vision detection (confidence > 0.5) for precise card
  boundaries, falling back to live-stream rect then static guide
- Vision rect maps directly to full image coords (no cover-mode offset
  needed), fixing the crop mismatch
- Live stream detection improved: uses gradient magnitude (edge detection)
  instead of raw brightness thresholding for better card boundary detection
- Configured for card aspect ratios (0.55–0.85), min 10% frame coverage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

BULL RUSH: $3000 deployed, ramp up everything

Config:
- Position size 25% → 30% (~$900/trade)
- Max positions 3 → 4 (deploy up to $3600)
- Confidence threshold 0.60 → 0.55 (more trades)
- Take profit 4x → 5x ATR (bigger targets to clear fees)
- Stops tighter 2x → 1.8x ATR (cut losers fast)
- Trailing activation 0.8R → 1.0R (let winners run)
- Time stop 72h → 96h (more patience)
- Min hold 60m → 30m, cooldown 30m → 15m
- Scan interval 10min → 5min

Brain: aggressive bias toward action, stop closing winners early
State: reset starting_value for $3000 deposit

Fee reality: 0.60% maker / 1.20% taker (Intro 1 tier)
Round trip: 1.20% (limit-limit) or 1.80% (limit-market)
